steps.title: Fastest path: mark the figure first
steps.content: Mark the circle facts first: radii are equal, a diameter sets up a right angle, a tangent is perpendicular to the radius, and central/inscribed angles connect to arcs.

steps.title: Use the circumference formula
steps.content: For a circle, circumference equals $\pi$ times diameter: $$C=\pi d.$$ The problem gives $C=76\pi$, so $$76\pi=\pi d.$$

steps.title: Cancel pi
steps.content: Divide both sides by $\pi$: $$d=76.$$ This is faster than finding the radius first because the formula already uses diameter.

steps.title: Confirm the diameter
steps.content: If the diameter is $76$, then the circumference is $\pi(76)=76\pi$, matching the given circumference. The diameter is 76 centimeters.
